briefing.headline: Cover water — summer clear conditions reward moving baits that trigger reaction strikes.
briefing.gameplan:
In summer with clear water and post frontal conditions, moving baits presentations shine.

Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better action

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Keep paddle tail swimbaits near the bottom — fishing too far off bottom is the biggest mistake; use lighter line (12-lb fluoro) and heavier head to achieve deeper running depth and better act
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Rig the Freeloader on a VMC Hard Ball Jighead; cast, let it fall toward the fish, and shake it over them — it triggers pressured ledge fish that have seen thousands of lures
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Offshore spinnerbait: 3/4oz, trim wire down a third, single willow blade. Cast, let helicopter down to bottom on tight line. Rip up sharply, let flutter back down. Provokes reaction strikes from suspended bass in summer heat.'
Wheeler: Best offshore spots included areas adjacent to grass lines, actual holes in the grass, and hard-bottomed shell beds in 8-35 feet of water.
Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, fish a mix of rock, sand, and weed in 15 feet or less. Hit as many spots as you can and hope the right ones bite.

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal summer fish retreat to reliable offshore structure at 12-25ft. They're less aggressive but still need to eat. Focus on primary structure — main lake points, ledges, and humps with shade or current.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize lures and switch to brighter colors
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
